items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation.

items.title: Amasty patches 25 Magento extensions, 1 critical
items.summary: Amasty released a massive security update for 25 extensions, with one critical vulnerability that could lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Critical vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento (PolyShell variant)
items.summary: Sansec discovered an unauthenticated PHP object injection flaw in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. CISA added this actively exploited flaw to its KEV catalog on June 3, 2026.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization and Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerabilities
items.summary: Adobe Commerce versions are affected by an Incorrect Authorization vulnerability leading to security feature bypass and unauthorized write access, and an SSRF vulnerability allowing security feature bypass and unauthorized read access.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable polyglot files, bypassing validation and potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This security update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.
